Event

Becker's Healthcare 15th Annual Meeting

April 29, 2025
Chicago, IL

 This year Becker’s Healthcare 15th Annual Meeting is happening  in Chicago, bringing together leaders to tackle healthcare’s biggest challenges—value-based care, cybersecurity, clinician burnout, and AI integration. Over four days of expert discussions, industry innovators will share strategies to drive operational excellence and enhance patient care.

We’ll be there—let’s connect to collaborate and shape the future of healthcare together.

RSVP to our happy hour

Unwind and connect with fellow healthcare leaders at BPD’s Happy Hour during Becker’s 15th Annual Meeting. We’ll be at Avli on the Park on Tuesday, April 29 from 6–8:30pm—just a short 10-minute walk from the conference. Come for the conversation, stay for the company.

Let us know if you’ll be joining by emailing us at info@bpdhealthcare.com. 

We’d love to see you there!

April 29, 2025, 2:10 PM - 2:50 PM CT

Strategic Growth from a Marketing Perspective

The Strategic Growth from a Marketing Perspective panel will delve into how hospital executives can leverage targeted marketing strategies to fuel sustainable growth and brand development.

Topics will include utilizing data-driven marketing to attract and retain patients, building strong community relationships, and differentiating services in an increasingly competitive healthcare landscape. Panelists will also explore the integration of digital platforms, social media, and patient experience in shaping a hospital's reputation and expanding its reach. Additionally, the discussion will highlight best practices for aligning marketing efforts with organizational goals to ensure measurable and long-term success.

Featured Speakers

Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer, BPD Andrew Chang Chief Marketing Officer, UChicago Medicine Sharon Line Clary SVP, Public Affairs, AdventHealth Ryan Younger Vice President, Marketing
Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer, BPD LinkedIn
Chris has provided leadership and guidance in the areas of strategy, brand, marketing, digital, and change management to hospitals and health systems across the country for more than 20 years. Chris is an award-winning author of six books, including Joe Public Doesn’t Care About Your Hospital, which became a field guide for driving transformation in hospital marketing departments across the country. He is a frequent keynote speaker and featured presenter on healthcare marketing and branding topics. Before joining Revive, Chris was the owner and lead strategist of Interval, a Minneapolis-based healthcare marketing firm he founded in 1995.  
Andrew Chang Chief Marketing Officer, UChicago Medicine LinkedIn
Andrew Chang serves as the Chief Marketing Officer at the University of Chicago Medicine. Previously, he held the position of Vice President of Marketing at VillageMD via acquisition of Summit Health/CityMD. His career has also seen him in roles such as Executive Director of Marketing and Physician Outreach at Piedmont Healthcare, along with leadership roles at UPS, and AirTran Airways and Southwest Airlines.  
Sharon Line Clary SVP, Public Affairs, AdventHealth LinkedIn
haron Line Clary is a graduate of Florida State University with a bachelor’s degree in Sports Information, Management, and Marketing and a master’s degree in Marketing and Communication Technologies. Her experience includes positions within sports, hospitality, and healthcare field including stints with the Florida State University Athletic and Sports Information Departments, World Cup Soccer, Olympic Games, and Mission Inn Resort and Club. For the past 24 years, Line Clary has been with AdventHealth. Currently, she is Senior Vice President of Community Impact & Partnership Engagement, where she oversees strategic marketing, sports/entertainment partnerships and clinical/corporate sales for the nationally ranked health care system which encompasses 9 hospitals, 14 emergency rooms, 30+ urgent care centers, outpatient facilities/services, a 600+ employed multi-specialty physician practice and 2,600+ medical staff.  
Ryan Younger Vice President, Marketing LinkedIn
